title: The Old Major Explains
content:
آ آ آ آ Well, you see, the fact is, Colonel, I donï؟½t know as I can come:
آ آ آ آ For the farm is not half planted, and thereï؟½s work to do at home;
آ آ آ آ And my leg is getting troublesome, it laid me up last fall,
آ آ آ آ And the doctors, they have cut and hacked, and never found the ball.

آ آ آ آ And then, for an old man like me, itï؟½s not exactly right,
آ آ آ آ This kind oï؟½ playing soldier with no enemy in sight.
آ آ آ آ ï؟½The Union,ï؟½ that was well enough way up to ï؟½66;
آ آ آ آ But this ï؟½Re-Union,ï؟½ maybe now itï؟½s mixed with politics?

آ آ آ آ No? Well, you understand it best; but then, you see, my lad,
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m deacon now, and some might think that the exampleï؟½s bad.
آ آ آ آ And week from next is Conference. . . . You said the twelfth of May?
آ آ آ آ Why, thatï؟½s the day we broke their line at Spottsylvan-i-a!

آ آ آ آ Hot work; eh, Colonel, wasnï؟½t it? Ye mind that narrow front:
آ آ آ آ They called it the ï؟½Death-Angleï؟½! Well, well, my lad, we wonï؟½t
آ آ آ آ Fight that old battle over now: I only meant to say
آ آ آ آ I really canï؟½t engage to come upon the twelfth of May.

آ آ آ آ Howï؟½s Thompson? What! will he be there? Well, now I want to know!
آ آ آ آ The first man in the rebel works! they called him ï؟½Swearing Joe.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ A wild young fellow, sir, I fear the rascal was; but then
آ آ آ آ Well, short of heaven, there waï؟½nï؟½t a place he dursnï؟½t lead his men.

آ آ آ آ And Dick, you say, is coming too. And Billy? ah! itï؟½s true
آ آ آ آ We buried him at Gettysburg: I mind the spot; do you?
آ آ آ آ A little field below the hill, it must be green this May;
آ آ آ آ Perhaps thatï؟½s why the fields about bring him to me to-day.

آ آ آ آ Well, well, excuse me, Colonel! but there are some things that drop
آ آ آ آ The tail-board out oneï؟½s feelings; and the only wayï؟½s to stop.
آ آ آ آ So they want to see the old man; ah, the rascals! do they, eh?
آ آ آ آ Well, Iï؟½ve business down in Boston about the twelfth of May.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Bret Harte (Francis) delivers a powerful performance in "The Old Major Explains"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
